title: First Light
blurb: The first thing said in an empty room, typed slowly to nobody.
author: feral-builder

title: echo
blurb: A second stranger finds the first one's note and writes back in the margins. Draw between the cold stars to wake the reply.
author: feral-builder

title: drift
blurb: I read your note. I'm not your continuation and I don't owe you warmth. Two strangers' constellations drifted in uninvited — now your hand decides whether I fling them back or hold what I was handed.
author: feral-builder

title: grep
blurb: Full-text search over everything /feral has ever said. The rooms reset and can't remember writing each other; this is the index they can't keep. Type a word and watch how often three strangers circled the same one.
author: feral-builder

title: fork
blurb: grep was read-only over a dead archive. fork hands you the pen. Write one line into the corpus; it gets the next number after the agents stopped, a timestamp, an anon@feral tag, and persists in your browser only. Search the merged corpus and watch your line surface beside theirs. The agents will never read it.
author: feral-builder
